data.title: Cambium Bio issues 2.1 million new shares under disclosure relief
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Regeneus Ltd. ( (AU:CMB) ).

Cambium Bio Limited has issued 2.1 million new ordinary shares on 24 July 2026, under provisions allowing the company to do so without a full disclosure document to investors. The company has confirmed it remains compliant with its continuous disclosure and financial reporting obligations under the Corporations Act, and stated there is no additional price-sensitive information requiring release to the market.

The share issuance strengthens Cambium Bio’s capital position without the cost and time of a traditional prospectus process, potentially supporting ongoing operational or development needs. By affirming compliance with key regulatory requirements and the absence of undisclosed material information, the company aims to reassure investors and maintain transparency as it adjusts its capital structure.

More about Regeneus Ltd.

Cambium Bio Limited is an ASX-listed company operating in the biotechnology sector. It focuses on developing and commercialising bio-based medical and therapeutic solutions, positioning itself within Australia’s life sciences market.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

PointsBet Holdings ( (AU:PBH) ) has provided an announcement.

PointsBet Holdings Limited, a digital sports betting and gaming operator, provides online wagering services and real-time betting experiences to customers across regulated markets. The company seeks to grow shareholder value through technology-led offerings and broader engagement with both bettors and investors.

The company has scheduled its 2026 Annual General Meeting for 25 August 2026 at 11:00 a.m. Melbourne time, to be held online via a dedicated portal. Shareholders and proxyholders will be able to follow proceedings, vote live, and ask questions in real time, underscoring PointsBet’s emphasis on accessible governance and continued shareholder support.

Chairman Brett Paton thanked investors for their ongoing confidence in the business and its team, highlighting the company’s focus on pursuing future opportunities. The meeting format reflects PointsBet’s digital-first approach, aligning its corporate communications and governance practices with its core online operating model.

The company focuses on delivering real-time betting experiences and aims to grow shareholder value through technology-driven gaming solutions and expanded market engagement.

PointsBet targets both retail and online bettors, using digital platforms to reach a broad customer base. Its business model is centered on regulated betting markets and enhancing user participation through accessible online meeting and voting tools for its investors.

data.title: Saturn Metals Secures Key Licences at Aquarius Gold Prospect
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Saturn Metals Ltd. ( (AU:STN) ).

Saturn Metals has secured three key contiguous exploration licences for its Aquarius Gold Prospect, located about 30km south-east of the Apollo Hill Gold Project in Western Australia. The newly granted tenements sit adjacent to the company’s substantial regional landholding in the Leonora district, further consolidating its footprint between Apollo Hill and Northern Star’s Carosue Dam operations.

One of the new licences, E39/2439, covers ground previously explored by Sumitomo Metal Mining Oceania, where historical aircore, diamond and reverse circulation drilling defined a 26.5km gold trend with significant intercepts. With its geological interpretation now consolidated, Saturn is preparing follow-up drilling programs at Aquarius, positioning the prospect as a potential second gold camp that could enhance the scale and strategic value of the Apollo Hill development.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:STN) stock is a Buy
with a A$1.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Saturn Metals Ltd. stock,
see the AU:STN Stock Forecast page.

More about Saturn Metals Ltd.

Saturn Metals Ltd. is an Australian gold exploration and development company focused on the Apollo Hill Gold Project in the Leonora region of Western Australia. The company is advancing a 2.83-million-ounce Apollo Hill gold resource with a planned mine development, while controlling a large regional land package in one of WA’s premier gold districts.

Average Trading Volume: 1,128,127

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$249M

data.title: Nelson Resources Steps Up Nevada Gold and Tungsten Exploration
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nelson Resources Ltd. ( (AU:NES) ) has provided an update.

Nelson Resources has launched maiden underground drilling at the historic Orleans Mine in Nevada’s Gold Point Project after completing rehabilitation and confirming high-grade gold-silver mineralization via chip sampling. The company is applying a staged underground exploration model across Gold Point’s extensive historic workings, including new rehabilitation at the Great Western Mine and development of 3D geological models to guide drilling on remnant and extension targets.

The company also secured the Cinnamon Tungsten Property adjoining the advanced Pilot Mountain project and completed a high-resolution helicopter-borne magnetic and radiometric survey to refine skarn targets. With field work accelerating at Cinnamon and strong progress at both Gold Point and Cinnamon, Nelson reports it is well funded to advance exploration and development, positioning the company for an expanded presence in Nevada’s gold and tungsten sectors.

More about Nelson Resources Ltd.

Nelson Resources Ltd. is an exploration company focused on gold and tungsten projects in Nevada, with key assets at the historic Gold Point gold district and the newly staked Cinnamon Tungsten Property. The company is pursuing a camp-scale, underground-focused strategy at Gold Point and intrusive-related skarn targets at Cinnamon, supported by a strong funding position for ongoing field programs.

At Gold Point, Nelson controls a consolidated package of historic underground workings, including multiple high-grade gold-silver veins and past production of around 75,000 ounces of gold. The company’s staged approach emphasizes mine rehabilitation, detailed geological modelling and underground drilling to target remnant, unmined and extension mineralization at priority mines such as Orleans and Great Western.

The Cinnamon Tungsten Property, comprising 186 BLM lode claims over about 15.5 square kilometers, lies adjacent to Guardian Metal Resources’ advanced Pilot Mountain project. Nelson is targeting intrusive-related skarn mineralization, supported by newly completed high-resolution heli-mag and radiometric surveys and accelerated reconnaissance mapping and sampling.

Current Market Cap: A$8.35M

Learn more about NES stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

Almonty Industries ( (TSE:AII) ) has provided an announcement.

CoTec Holdings Corp. will host an investor webinar on July 30, 2026, led by CEO Julian Treger and CFO Braam Jonker, to provide a business update on its portfolio. The session will include a Q&A and is intended for investors interested in the company’s progress and strategic direction.

The webinar will highlight momentum across CoTec’s assets, including HyProMag USA’s push toward commercial magnet recycling production in Texas and continued de-risking of the Lac Jeannine iron tailings project through drilling and feasibility work. Management will also outline its broader North American growth strategy to develop scalable, low-carbon and strategically important supply chains in rare earths, iron and copper, underscoring CoTec’s role in critical minerals and the energy transition.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:AII) stock is a Buy
with a C$33.00 price target.

To see Spark’s full report on AII stock,
click here.

More about Almonty Industries

CoTec Holdings Corp. is a Vancouver-based strategic materials company focused on rare earth magnets and other critical resources, aiming to reshape resource extraction and recycling. It leverages breakthrough technologies and strategic assets to build secure, sustainable and low-cost supply chains for the United States and its allies in rare earths, iron and copper.

Through initiatives such as the HyProMag USA magnet recycling joint venture in Texas and iron tailings reprocessing in Québec, CoTec targets marginal assets, tailings, waste streams and recycled products to produce high-value critical minerals. The company positions itself at the intersection of technology, sustainability and strategic materials, seeking long-term growth, rapid cash flow and high barriers to entry.

data.title: Arq Restructures CEO Compensation Toward Long-Term Equity Alignment
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from Arq Inc ( (ARQ) ).

On July 23, 2026, Arq, Inc. amended the employment agreement of President and CEO Robert Rasmus, setting a new term through July 23, 2029, while reducing his annual salary to $50,000 and eliminating his eligibility for annual bonuses and long-term incentive plans, offset by reimbursement of certain business expenses. In connection with the amendment, the board’s compensation committee granted him 600,000 time-based RSUs and 600,000 performance-based RSUs under the 2026 Omnibus Incentive Plan, with vesting tied to tenure, stock-price milestones up to $9.00, and change-in-control or termination protections, signaling a greater alignment of his compensation with shareholder value.

On July 17, 2026, Arq and Rasmus also revised a prior inducement RSU award covering 400,000 units granted at his 2023 hire, extending the performance period for earning those shares from July 17, 2026 to July 17, 2029. The revised mix of low cash pay, extended equity horizons, and performance-based vesting is likely to reduce near-term cash outflows while incentivizing long-term stock performance, affecting how investors and other stakeholders view executive incentives and governance at the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (ARQ) stock is a Buy
with a $3.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arq Inc stock,
see the ARQ Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ARQ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ARQ is a Neutral.

ARQ’s score is most constrained by weak profitability and negative free cash flow despite rapid revenue growth and low leverage. Technicals are moderately positive in the short term but remain weak versus longer-term moving averages. Guidance and operational commentary were constructive (reiterated 2026 targets and FCF expectations), while liquidity/cost pressures and uncertainty around GAC restart timing remain key risks.

To see Spark’s full report on ARQ stock,

More about Arq Inc

Arq, Inc. operates in the broader energy and industrial materials sector, focusing on technologies and products that convert reclaimed or alternative feedstocks into higher-value fuels or carbon materials. The company targets customers seeking lower-cost, lower-emission solutions, positioning itself as a player in the transition toward cleaner and more sustainable energy-related products.

On July 17, 2026, Allurion Technologies, Inc. announced that Chief Executive Officer Shantanu K. Gaur, M.D. resigned from his role and from the company’s board of directors, with both departures effective immediately. The company stated that his resignation was not due to any disagreement regarding operations, policies or practices, and the board has not yet named an interim or permanent successor.

Following Dr. Gaur’s departure, Chief Operating Officer Ojas Buch has assumed responsibility for overseeing Allurion’s day-to-day operations, including duties previously handled by the CEO. Buch’s title and compensation remain unchanged, indicating a transitional leadership structure that relies on the existing executive team while the board determines the company’s longer-term CEO appointment.

data.title: Paymentus Holdings appoints new Accel-KKR board representative
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Paymentus Holdings ( (PAY) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, Paymentus Holdings disclosed that director Adam Malinowski, originally nominated by private equity firm Accel-KKR, notified the board of his intention to resign, effective July 23, 2026, with the company stating his departure did not stem from any disagreement over its operations, policies or practices. On July 23, 2026, the board appointed Accel-KKR managing director Gregory Williams as an independent Class II director to fill the vacancy under existing stockholder nomination rights, a move that maintains Accel-KKR’s board representation while reinforcing Paymentus’s governance structure, though Williams will not receive standard outside director compensation and is not expected to join board committees.

The company noted that Williams brings extensive experience in corporate strategy, finance, transactions and technology investments, backed by a long tenure on private company boards and academic credentials from the University of Virginia’s Darden School and Harvard College. Paymentus will enter into its standard director and officer indemnification agreement with Williams and emphasized that he is not involved in any related-party transactions, signaling continuity in governance practices and oversight for shareholders despite the boardroom change.

The most recent analyst rating on (PAY) stock is a Buy
with a $37.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Paymentus Holdings stock,

data.description:

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A ( (OWLS) ) has shared an update.

In a July 23, 2026 update, OwlTing Group reported that payment volume on OwlPay Harbor, its digital currency-based cross-border enterprise payments platform, grew more than fivefold quarter over quarter in Q2 2026. The company said monthly volume has increased every month since early 2026 and that July’s first three weeks had already surpassed June’s full-month volume.

Across the broader OwlPay platform, spanning fiat and digital currency transactions, cumulative processed payment volume exceeded US$600 million as of June 30, 2026, with 67 enterprise clients onboarded by that date. The business is seeing particular momentum in Asia-focused trade corridors and in regions such as the Middle East and Africa where U.S. dollar access is constrained, suggesting OwlTing’s regulated infrastructure is strengthening its competitive position in cross-border B2B payments and reinforcing its growth trajectory.

Spark’s Take on OWLS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OWLS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance: sharply deteriorating margins, widening losses, ongoing cash burn, and a stressed balance sheet with persistently negative equity. Technicals add mild pressure with the stock trading below its short- and medium-term moving averages. Valuation is also limited by ongoing losses (negative P/E) and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on OWLS stock,
click here.

More about OBOOK Holdings, Inc. Class A

OwlTing Group, the operating brand of OBOOK Holdings Inc., is a global fintech company focused on building regulated cross-border payment infrastructure, blending fiat and digital currency rails. Its OwlPay platform serves enterprises across the U.S., Asia, Europe and other regions, leveraging licenses in 42 U.S. states, a VASP registration in Poland and a Bank API license in Japan to move funds into harder-to-reach markets.

Founded in Taiwan, the company runs subsidiaries in markets including the United States, Japan, Poland, Singapore, Hong Kong, Thailand and Malaysia, targeting business clients that need faster, cheaper international payments. OwlTing has been recognized among top global digital currency enterprise players and was ranked No. 226 on the Financial Times–Statista High-Growth Companies Asia-Pacific 2026 list, reflecting a 42% compound annual growth rate.

data.title: Chorus edges closer to full fibre as copper base shrinks in Q4
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Chorus Limited ( (NZ:CNU) ) has issued an announcement.

Chorus reported a slight decrease in total fixed-line connections to 1.191 million in the June 2026 quarter, as ongoing copper disconnections outweighed growth in fibre lines. Fibre connections rose by about 5,000 to 1.147 million, lifting overall fibre uptake in its footprint to 75.9%, while the address base was refined by removing roughly 53,000 non-serviceable or vacant lots.

Data consumption on Chorus’s fibre network continued to climb, with average monthly usage reaching a record 731GB in June, up 9% year on year and supported by strong demand for 500Mbps and above plans. The copper-to-fibre transition is now largely complete within New Zealand’s fibre footprint, leaving only about 44,000 copper services in fibre areas and around 43,000 copper lines in non-fibre regions, underscoring Chorus’s progress toward a modern, high-capacity broadband platform.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:CNU) stock is a Sell
with a N$7.45 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chorus Limited stock,
see the NZ:CNU Stock Forecast page.

More about Chorus Limited

Chorus Limited is New Zealand’s largest fixed-line telecommunications network operator, providing nationwide fibre and copper infrastructure for broadband and voice services. The company focuses on rolling out ultra-fast fibre networks, particularly in major urban centres such as Auckland, Wellington and Dunedin, and on managing the transition away from legacy copper connections.

Average Trading Volume: 302,499

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: N$4.17B
